subject: Accounting
title: Special Order Pricing
description: A 60-second lesson on Special Order Pricing.

hook.text: A hotel chain offers to buy 5,000 pillows at $8 each. Your normal price is $15. Accept or refuse?

buildup.text: Special order analysis asks: will this order contribute to profit, even at a lower price?

discovery.text: If the price exceeds variable cost and there's spare capacity, the order adds to profit.

twist.text: But if regular customers learn about the discount, they'll demand the same price—eroding margins.

climax.text: The decision depends on capacity, variable costs, and whether the deal stays confidential.

punchline.text: Below list price can still mean above zero profit.

quiz.question: When should a company accept a special order below normal price?
options: When the price exceeds variable cost and spare capacity exists
